title: Madhuca brochidodroma
taxonomy: Madhuca brochidodroma belongs to the family Sapotaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a type of tree that is native to the tropical forests of Asia. The classification of Madhuca brochidodroma is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the order Ebenales. This plant is closely related to other Sapotaceae species, such as the Indian butter tree. The specific classification of Madhuca brochidodroma is still under study and research.

The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include treating various conditions such as inflammation, fever, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
